paper_map.title: Duplex microstructure enhanced mechanical property and underlined mechanism in Fe35MnxAl0.1C low-density steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: The microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-35Mn-xAl-0.1C (x = 4, 6, 8, 10 wt%) low-density steels were investigated. With increasing Al content, the matrix changed from single austenite to ferrite-based duplex, and the 8Al steel achieved an excellent strength-ductility combination (YS 552 MPa, UTS 740 MPa, UE 31%). Detailed TEM analysis revealed that the activation of edge dislocations and dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) in ferrite grains, confined by austenite, significantly enhanced the strain hardening rate. This work proposes a new ductility-enhancing mechanism for BCC-based low-density duplex steels.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the dislocation patterns and work hardening mechanisms in ferrite-based duplex Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel, particularly the role of edge dislocations and dynamic slip band refinement, in order to understand the enhanced ductility of the 8Al steel.
material_systems: Fe-35Mn-8Al-0.1C (8Al) – ferrite-based duplex steel with DO3 ordered domains
Fe-35Mn-10Al-0.1C (10Al) – nearly fully ferritic steel with B2/DO3 ordered phases
Key phases: austenite (γ), ferrite (α), DO3 ordered domains, B2 ordered phases
main_process_variables: Hot forging to φ 16.5 mm rods
Solution treatment at 1050 °C for 2 h followed by oil quenching
Tensile deformation at room temperature (from 0 to ~30% engineering strain)

sample_aliases.meaning: Fe-35Mn-10Al-0.1C steel (nearly fully ferritic lightweight steel)
